message:
fix(channel): protect broadcast recv from u64→usize truncation on 32-bit targets

Replace the bare `saturating_sub(earliest) as usize` cast with
`usize::try_from(delta)` wrapped in an `if let Ok(offset)` guard.
On 32-bit targets, a large next_index delta (> u32::MAX) would
silently truncate and incorrectly index into the front of the ring
buffer, returning a stale message instead of pending.

With the checked conversion, an out-of-range delta safely falls
through to the Pending/Closed check path, matching the expected
behavior when the receiver cursor is ahead of the current window.

Also adds a `#[cfg(target_pointer_width = "32")]` regression test
that sets next_index past u32::MAX and verifies the receiver
returns Pending instead of a spurious message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
